title: THIS Is Who Students Think Attacked Us on 9/11?!
body:
This IMO is one of the best YAF presentations of the “campus interviews” I’ve seen. It gave me the impression that these are serious students, not of one demographic, who can’t answer the question and understand that they should be able to. You allowed them to come across as respectfully regretting that educators let them down.
Thank you.
There is real impact in this.
author: @khfoust1

title: THIS Is Who Students Think Attacked Us on 9/11?!
body: In defense of these “college” kids, they don’t even know what gender they are. How in the hell are they supposed to know what happened 20 years ago.

body: 9/11 was probably the most horrific thing to happen in America and after just 20 years it’s forgotten about by young people.  WOW.   I live on a tiny island in the middle of the Irish Sea and can remember it like it was yesterday.

title: THIS Is Who Students Think Attacked Us on 9/11?!
body:
I realized in 6th grade that some of the teachers were "not too bright".
I was always the one kid in class who withdrew a whole stack of interesting books from the library.
I realized that educating myself was MY RESPONSIBILITY.
I didn't wait for someone to "tell" me anything, I went forth and found out for myself.
PEOPLE NEED TO LEARN TO ASK QUESTIONS.
author: @BjornBaker-k3j

title: THIS Is Who Students Think Attacked Us on 9/11?!

title: George Mason University 🎓 Fairfax County, VA📍#salarytransparentstreet #salarytransparency
body: People are so shocked the artist couldn’t think of a salary but that’s just the reality of being an artist in general. Artists work off of clients and commissions and if they do work for like a company or studio, the starting rate is like 15k at most. So saying 1.7k a month is actually pretty reasonable when you think about it.

title: George Mason University 🎓 Fairfax County, VA📍#salarytransparentstreet #salarytransparency
body: I got my degree in psychology and my first job paid very little maybe about 25k and I was dealing with a really tough population. I currently work in psychiatric research at a private university and still make less than 40k. The only nice thing about where I work now is that the people are chill and the doctors I work with are nice and also I have and office with a door that automatically locks😅
author: @mikailagray

title: 10 Things to Know Before Going to George Mason University
body: from a person who grew up in Fairfax from pre-k to senior year, there are things to do in Fairfax you just have to know what you are looking for. second, if you like the city feel but dont like being in traffic all the time fairfax is good because its usually just a 30 min metro ride into DC if you want to go to museums or art exhibits or clubs, so in my opinion its better that way since you dont get all of the congestion from people trying to go to the museums and instead just get the city vibe. (also the metro is pretty safe you just have to be vigalent and avoid the people who look like bad news
author: @imapotato3038

body: I like how the majority of George Masons Undergrads fail to utilize the fraternities when partying or seeking for social environments. title: Day in the life of a #bioinformatician working in LONDON 🇬🇧 🧬 👩🏻‍💻
body:
Hii i love your channel so far 🫶🏻
I was actually looking into this career so I just had some questions to ask you if that's okay!
1. How would you recommend I build a portfolio? (As in, what type of projects, workshops, etc.)
2. How much of it is actual coding vs. analyzing data/software in general or more working with researchers?
3. Was it difficult to find a job in this field or was it relatively easy?
4. What's the overall process academically?

Take your time with your replies. I appreciate your videos btw, they're so fun to watch, and so informative, too!
author: @30-viiisanika83

title: Day in the life of a #bioinformatician working in LONDON 🇬🇧 🧬 👩🏻‍💻
body:

I would like to ask you a couple of questions if possible:
Is the study workload very demanding?
Regarding employment, is it easy to find a job quickly, or did you face difficulties if you are currently employed? ❤

title: George Mason University Campus Tour
body: I remember when the University College of Virginia, a four-year college much-needed in Northern Virginia, received its university status in 1972.  Now, students in the region didn't have to go all the way to Richmond, or pay the huge fees for the universities in Washington, DC.   I wanted to go there but, as usual at that time, I had little or no money and was already deciding on a meal or a bus ticket to work.  It has grown from its small campus in Fairfax, Virginia to the incredible university it is today.
author: @ColonelMarcellus

title: George Mason University Campus Tour
body: This is my dream college, it’s soo diverse and it’s so beautiful seeing people from all over the world just uniting and enjoying there time.
author: @loomaaldamiri6551

title: George Mason University Campus Tour
body: It's a lot bigger than when I saw it in the early 1970s.  The dorms hadn't been built yet!  It didn't get University status (by an act of the Virginia General Assembly) until 1972, but was a respected school even when it was part of the University of Virginia.  It was in the City of Fairfax, Virginia.   Now they have multiple campuses including, I think, one in South Korea?
author: @ColonelMarcellus
